    "filePath": "/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1767099565938/docs/case-studies/issue-1039/02-ROOT-CAUSES.md",
    "oldString": "### 1. Playwright MCP Configuration Issue\n\n#### The Problem\n\nThe Playwright MCP server was configured without explicit timeout settings. When the AI clicked the \"Load data\" button, it triggered an operation that caused the browser to become busy (loading 300K+ GeoJSON points), and Playwright's `click()` action never completed.\n\n#### Evidence\n\nFrom the log initialization:\n```\n[2025-12-30T11:20:34.892Z] [INFO] 🎭 Playwright MCP detected - enabling browser automation hints\n```\n\nThe tools list shows Playwright MCP was connected:\n```json\n{\n  \"name\": \"playwright\",\n  \"status\": \"connected\"\n}\n```\n\nHowever, there's no indication of configured timeouts.\n\n#### The Default Behavior\n\nAccording to [Playwright documentation](https://playwright.dev/docs/api/class-timeouterror):\n- Default action timeout: 5000ms (5 seconds)\n- Default navigation timeout: 60000ms (60 seconds)\n\nBut these defaults can be overridden by Playwright MCP configuration.\n\n#### The Fix\n\nConfigure Playwright MCP with explicit timeouts:\n```bash\nclaude mcp add playwright -s user -- npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest \\\n  --isolated --headless --no-sandbox \\\n  --timeout-action=600000 \\\n  --viewport-size 1920x1080\n```",
    "newString": "### 1. Playwright MCP Timeout Not Being Respected\n\n#### The Problem\n\nThe Playwright MCP server **is** configured with explicit timeout settings (`--timeout-action=600000` = 10 minutes), but the timeout did not work as expected. When the AI clicked the \"Load data\" button, the action hung for **1 hour and 34 minutes** without triggering the configured timeout.\n\n#### Evidence\n\nFrom the installation script (`ubuntu-24-server-install.sh:1352`):\n```bash\nclaude mcp add playwright -s user -- npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest \\\n  --isolated --headless --no-sandbox \\\n  --timeout-action=600000 --viewport-size 1920x1080\n```\n\nThe timeout is configured as 600,000ms (10 minutes), but the operation hung for 94 minutes.\n\nFrom the log initialization:\n```\n[2025-12-30T11:20:34.892Z] [INFO] 🎭 Playwright MCP detected - enabling browser automation hints\n```\n\n#### Why the Timeout Didn't Work\n\nPossible reasons why `--timeout-action=600000` didn't trigger:\n\n1. **MCP Protocol Layer Issue**: The timeout might apply to the Playwright action, but the MCP protocol layer may not properly propagate the timeout error back to the client.\n\n2. **Browser JavaScript Blocking**: When JavaScript execution blocks the browser's main thread (as with loading 300K+ points), Playwright may not be able to detect the timeout condition.\n\n3. **MCP Connection Not Dropped**: The MCP connection remained active (no disconnect), so the client continued waiting indefinitely.\n\n4. **Action vs Connection Timeout**: The `--timeout-action` may only apply to action completion detection, not to the MCP response waiting.\n\n#### The Workaround\n\nSince the configured timeout doesn't appear to work reliably, additional safeguards are needed:\n\n1. **AI-side caution**: Avoid clicking buttons that trigger heavy operations\n2. **Client-side timeout**: Add timeout detection in solve.mjs\n3. **Use browser_evaluate**: For potentially long operations, use JavaScript with explicit timeouts instead of browser_click",
    "originalFile": "# Root Causes: Solve Command Stuck on Playwright MCP Tool Call\n\nThis document provides an in-depth analysis of the root causes that led to the solve command becoming stuck on a Playwright MCP tool call.\n\n## Summary of Root Causes\n\n| Priority | Root Cause | Impact | Mitigation |\n|----------|-----------|--------|------------|\n| **Critical** | No action timeout in Playwright MCP config | Session hangs indefinitely | Add `--timeout-action=600000` |\n| **High** | No client-side timeout for MCP tools | solve.mjs waits forever | Implement tool call timeout |\n| **Medium** | AI clicked button triggering heavy operation | Browser became unresponsive | AI prompt guidance |\n| **Low** | Missing error recovery mechanism | Manual intervention required | Add auto-recovery |\n\n## Detailed Analysis\n\n### 1. Playwright MCP Configuration Issue\n\n#### The Problem\n\nThe Playwright MCP server was configured without explicit timeout settings. When the AI clicked the \"Load data\" button, it triggered an operation that caused the browser to become busy (loading 300K+ GeoJSON points), and Playwright's `click()` action never completed.\n\n#### Evidence\n\nFrom the log initialization:\n```\n[2025-12-30T11:20:34.892Z] [INFO] 🎭 Playwright MCP detected - enabling browser automation hints\n```\n\nThe tools list shows Playwright MCP was connected:\n```json\n{\n  \"name\": \"playwright\",\n  \"status\": \"connected\"\n}\n```\n\nHowever, there's no indication of configured timeouts.\n\n#### The Default Behavior\n\nAccording to [Playwright documentation](https://playwright.dev/docs/api/class-timeouterror):\n- Default action timeout: 5000ms (5 seconds)\n- Default navigation timeout: 60000ms (60 seconds)\n\nBut these defaults can be overridden by Playwright MCP configuration.\n\n#### The Fix\n\nConfigure Playwright MCP with explicit timeouts:\n```bash\nclaude mcp add playwright -s user -- npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest \\\n  --isolated --headless --no-sandbox \\\n  --timeout-action=600000 \\\n  --viewport-size 1920x1080\n```\n\n---\n\n### 2. Heavy JavaScript Operation Blocked the Browser\n\n#### The Problem\n\nThe \"Load data\" button was designed to:\n1. Fetch `mrds.geojson` (107MB file)\n2. Parse 304,613 GeoJSON features\n3. Create Cesium entities for each point\n4. Render them on a 3D globe\n\nThis is a computationally intensive operation that can make the browser tab unresponsive.\n\n#### Evidence\n\nFrom the page snapshot before the click:\n```yaml\n- generic: \"Отображено точек: 0\" (Points displayed: 0)\n- generic: \"Всего в базе: 304,613\" (Total in database: 304,613)\n```\n\nThe page was showing 0 points, indicating the data hadn't been loaded yet. The button was supposed to trigger this loading.\n\n#### Why This Matters\n\nWhen JavaScript executes a heavy operation:\n1. The browser's main thread becomes blocked\n2. Playwright cannot receive acknowledgment that the click completed\n3. The `click()` action appears to hang indefinitely\n\n#### The Pattern\n\n```javascript\n// Typical problematic pattern in the target application\nbutton.addEventListener('click', async () => {\n  // This blocks the main thread\n  const data = await fetch('mrds.geojson');  // 107MB\n  const json = await data.json();            // Parse 300K features\n  json.features.forEach(feature => {         // Create 300K entities\n    viewer.entities.add(createEntity(feature));\n  });\n});\n```\n\n#### The Fix\n\nThe target application should use:\n- Web Workers for heavy parsing\n- Reque***********Frame for batched rendering\n- Progress indicators to keep UI responsive\n\nFor the solve command, the AI should:\n- Not click buttons that trigger heavy operations without safeguards\n- Use `browser_wait_for` with specific text expectations\n- Verify the operation completes with snapshots\n\n---\n\n### 3. Missing Timeout Detection in solve.mjs\n\n#### The Problem\n\nThe `solve.mjs` script doesn't implement timeout detection for MCP tool calls. When the Playwright MCP tool hangs, solve.mjs continues waiting indefinitely.\n\n#### Evidence\n\nThe log shows no timeout warning or error:\n```\n[2025-12-30T11:22:53.672Z] [INFO] {...browser_click call...}\n[2025-12-30T12:57:10.345Z] [INFO] 📁 Keeping directory...\n```\n\nThere's a 1h34m gap with no log entries - the system was simply waiting.\n\n#### The Expected Behavior\n\nsolve.mjs should:\n1. Track the start time of each tool call\n2. Implement a configurable timeout (e.g., 10 minutes for browser operations)\n3. Log warnings when operations take longer than expected\n4. Optionally kill and restart hung operations\n\n#### The Fix\n\nImplement timeout detection in solve.mjs:\n\n```javascript\n// Example implementation\nconst TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS = 600000; // 10 minutes\n\nasync function executeToolWithTimeout(tool, timeout = TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS) {\n  const startTime = Date.now();\n\n  const timeoutPromise = new Promise((_, reject) => {\n    setTimeout(() => {\n      reject(new Error(`Tool ${tool.name} timed out after ${timeout}ms`));\n    }, timeout);\n  });\n\n  const toolPromise = executeTool(tool);\n\n  try {\n    return await Promise.race([toolPromise, timeoutPromise]);\n  } catch (error) {\n    if (error.message.includes('timed out')) {\n      log(`⚠️ Tool ${tool.name} timed out, attempting recovery...`);\n      // Implement recovery logic\n    }\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 4. Known Playwright MCP Issues\n\n#### MCP Ping Timeout (Issue #982)\n\nFrom [microsoft/playwright-mcp#982](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp/issues/982):\n\n> \"When agents perform time-intensive operations (such as web scraping, form filling, waiting for page loads, or processing large datasets), they may fail to respond to MCP ping requests within the hardcoded 5-second timeout. The server interprets this as a dead connection and terminates the session.\"\n\nHowever, in our case, the connection wasn't terminated - it just hung. This suggests a different failure mode.\n\n#### browser_click Download Issues (Issue #355)\n\nFrom [microsoft/playwright-mcp#355](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp/issues/355):\n\n> \"Users have been facing issues with automating 'click to download' workflows with the playwright-mcp toolset. The browser_click tool is not able to trigger downloads on versions >= 0.0.19.\"\n\nWhile not directly applicable (the button wasn't triggering a download), this shows that `browser_click` has known issues with certain click actions.\n\n#### Variable Success Rate (Claude Code Issue #1383)\n\nFrom [anthropics/claude-code#1383](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/1383):\n\n> \"Playwright MCP success is... very variable. Sometimes it works great, it takes screenshots, it verifies its own work... then Claude is superbly useful. Other times it just can't seem to use playwright properly and so reverts to Curl.\"\n\nThis indicates that Playwright MCP reliability issues are a known problem.\n\n---\n\n### 5. AI Decision to Click Without Verification\n\n#### The Problem\n\nThe AI decided to click the \"Load data\" button without:\n1. Checking if the prerequisite data file existed\n2. Understanding the impact of loading 300K+ points\n3. Setting up any timeout or verification mechanism\n\n#### Evidence\n\nFrom the AI's message before the click:\n```\n\"Good! The page loaded. I can see there's a Cesium Ion 401 error (token issue)\nand a 404 for favicon. Let me click the 'Load Data' button to test data loading:\"\n```\n\nThe AI acknowledged errors but proceeded anyway.\n\n#### The Better Approach\n\nThe AI should have:\n1. Verified that `mrds.geojson` existed before clicking\n2. Noted that loading 304,613 points could be slow\n3. Used `browser_evaluate` to test data loading with a timeout\n4. Used `browser_wait_for` with a specific expected outcome\n\n#### The Fix\n\nAdd guidance to the AI system prompt:\n```markdown\nPlaywright MCP usage guidelines:\n- Before clicking buttons that load large datasets, verify the data source exists\n- For operations that may take long, use browser_evaluate with explicit timeouts\n- After clicks that trigger data loading, use browser_wait_for with expected text\n- Always have a fallback plan if browser operations don't complete in expected time\n```\n\n---\n\n## Root Cause Diagram\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                          SOLVE COMMAND STUCK                                 │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n                                    ▲\n                                    │\n              ┌─────────────────────┴─────────────────────┐\n              │                                           │\n              ▼                                           ▼\n┌─────────────────────────────┐           ┌─────────────────────────────┐\n│   Playwright MCP Config     │           │    solve.mjs Architecture   │\n│   (No timeout configured)   │           │    (No timeout detection)   │\n└─────────────────────────────┘           └─────────────────────────────┘\n              │                                           │\n              ▼                                           ▼\n┌─────────────────────────────┐           ┌─────────────────────────────┐\n│   browser_click hangs on    │           │   solve.mjs waits forever   │\n│   heavy JS operation        │           │   for tool response         │\n└─────────────────────────────┘           └─────────────────────────────┘\n              │                                           │\n              └───────────────────┬───────────────────────┘\n                                  │\n                                  ▼\n                    ┌─────────────────────────────┐\n                    │   No error, no timeout,     │\n                    │   manual Ctrl+C required    │\n                    └─────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThe root cause is a combination of factors:\n\n1. **Primary**: Missing timeout configuration in Playwright MCP\n2. **Secondary**: Heavy JavaScript operation blocking the browser\n3. **Tertiary**: No timeout detection in solve.mjs\n4. **Contributing**: AI clicking a button without proper verification\n\nAll of these issues contributed to the solve command becoming stuck for over 90 minutes with no error message or automatic recovery.\n",

    "originalFile": "# Root Causes: Solve Command Stuck on Playwright MCP Tool Call\n\nThis document provides an in-depth analysis of the root causes that led to the solve command becoming stuck on a Playwright MCP tool call.\n\n## Summary of Root Causes\n\n| Priority | Root Cause | Impact | Mitigation |\n|----------|-----------|--------|------------|\n| **Critical** | No action timeout in Playwright MCP config | Session hangs indefinitely | Add `--timeout-action=600000` |\n| **High** | No client-side timeout for MCP tools | solve.mjs waits forever | Implement tool call timeout |\n| **Medium** | AI clicked button triggering heavy operation | Browser became unresponsive | AI prompt guidance |\n| **Low** | Missing error recovery mechanism | Manual intervention required | Add auto-recovery |\n\n## Detailed Analysis\n\n### 1. Playwright MCP Timeout Not Being Respected\n\n#### The Problem\n\nThe Playwright MCP server **is** configured with explicit timeout settings (`--timeout-action=600000` = 10 minutes), but the timeout did not work as expected. When the AI clicked the \"Load data\" button, the action hung for **1 hour and 34 minutes** without triggering the configured timeout.\n\n#### Evidence\n\nFrom the installation script (`ubuntu-24-server-install.sh:1352`):\n```bash\nclaude mcp add playwright -s user -- npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest \\\n  --isolated --headless --no-sandbox \\\n  --timeout-action=600000 --viewport-size 1920x1080\n```\n\nThe timeout is configured as 600,000ms (10 minutes), but the operation hung for 94 minutes.\n\nFrom the log initialization:\n```\n[2025-12-30T11:20:34.892Z] [INFO] 🎭 Playwright MCP detected - enabling browser automation hints\n```\n\n#### Why the Timeout Didn't Work\n\nPossible reasons why `--timeout-action=600000` didn't trigger:\n\n1. **MCP Protocol Layer Issue**: The timeout might apply to the Playwright action, but the MCP protocol layer may not properly propagate the timeout error back to the client.\n\n2. **Browser JavaScript Blocking**: When JavaScript execution blocks the browser's main thread (as with loading 300K+ points), Playwright may not be able to detect the timeout condition.\n\n3. **MCP Connection Not Dropped**: The MCP connection remained active (no disconnect), so the client continued waiting indefinitely.\n\n4. **Action vs Connection Timeout**: The `--timeout-action` may only apply to action completion detection, not to the MCP response waiting.\n\n#### The Workaround\n\nSince the configured timeout doesn't appear to work reliably, additional safeguards are needed:\n\n1. **AI-side caution**: Avoid clicking buttons that trigger heavy operations\n2. **Client-side timeout**: Add timeout detection in solve.mjs\n3. **Use browser_evaluate**: For potentially long operations, use JavaScript with explicit timeouts instead of browser_click\n\n---\n\n### 2. Heavy JavaScript Operation Blocked the Browser\n\n#### The Problem\n\nThe \"Load data\" button was designed to:\n1. Fetch `mrds.geojson` (107MB file)\n2. Parse 304,613 GeoJSON features\n3. Create Cesium entities for each point\n4. Render them on a 3D globe\n\nThis is a computationally intensive operation that can make the browser tab unresponsive.\n\n#### Evidence\n\nFrom the page snapshot before the click:\n```yaml\n- generic: \"Отображено точек: 0\" (Points displayed: 0)\n- generic: \"Всего в базе: 304,613\" (Total in database: 304,613)\n```\n\nThe page was showing 0 points, indicating the data hadn't been loaded yet. The button was supposed to trigger this loading.\n\n#### Why This Matters\n\nWhen JavaScript executes a heavy operation:\n1. The browser's main thread becomes blocked\n2. Playwright cannot receive acknowledgment that the click completed\n3. The `click()` action appears to hang indefinitely\n\n#### The Pattern\n\n```javascript\n// Typical problematic pattern in the target application\nbutton.addEventListener('click', async () => {\n  // This blocks the main thread\n  const data = await fetch('mrds.geojson');  // 107MB\n  const json = await data.json();            // Parse 300K features\n  json.features.forEach(feature => {         // Create 300K entities\n    viewer.entities.add(createEntity(feature));\n  });\n});\n```\n\n#### The Fix\n\nThe target application should use:\n- Web Workers for heavy parsing\n- Reque***********Frame for batched rendering\n- Progress indicators to keep UI responsive\n\nFor the solve command, the AI should:\n- Not click buttons that trigger heavy operations without safeguards\n- Use `browser_wait_for` with specific text expectations\n- Verify the operation completes with snapshots\n\n---\n\n### 3. Missing Timeout Detection in solve.mjs\n\n#### The Problem\n\nThe `solve.mjs` script doesn't implement timeout detection for MCP tool calls. When the Playwright MCP tool hangs, solve.mjs continues waiting indefinitely.\n\n#### Evidence\n\nThe log shows no timeout warning or error:\n```\n[2025-12-30T11:22:53.672Z] [INFO] {...browser_click call...}\n[2025-12-30T12:57:10.345Z] [INFO] 📁 Keeping directory...\n```\n\nThere's a 1h34m gap with no log entries - the system was simply waiting.\n\n#### The Expected Behavior\n\nsolve.mjs should:\n1. Track the start time of each tool call\n2. Implement a configurable timeout (e.g., 10 minutes for browser operations)\n3. Log warnings when operations take longer than expected\n4. Optionally kill and restart hung operations\n\n#### The Fix\n\nImplement timeout detection in solve.mjs:\n\n```javascript\n// Example implementation\nconst TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS = 600000; // 10 minutes\n\nasync function executeToolWithTimeout(tool, timeout = TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS) {\n  const startTime = Date.now();\n\n  const timeoutPromise = new Promise((_, reject) => {\n    setTimeout(() => {\n      reject(new Error(`Tool ${tool.name} timed out after ${timeout}ms`));\n    }, timeout);\n  });\n\n  const toolPromise = executeTool(tool);\n\n  try {\n    return await Promise.race([toolPromise, timeoutPromise]);\n  } catch (error) {\n    if (error.message.includes('timed out')) {\n      log(`⚠️ Tool ${tool.name} timed out, attempting recovery...`);\n      // Implement recovery logic\n    }\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 4. Known Playwright MCP Issues\n\n#### MCP Ping Timeout (Issue #982)\n\nFrom [microsoft/playwright-mcp#982](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp/issues/982):\n\n> \"When agents perform time-intensive operations (such as web scraping, form filling, waiting for page loads, or processing large datasets), they may fail to respond to MCP ping requests within the hardcoded 5-second timeout. The server interprets this as a dead connection and terminates the session.\"\n\nHowever, in our case, the connection wasn't terminated - it just hung. This suggests a different failure mode.\n\n#### browser_click Download Issues (Issue #355)\n\nFrom [microsoft/playwright-mcp#355](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp/issues/355):\n\n> \"Users have been facing issues with automating 'click to download' workflows with the playwright-mcp toolset. The browser_click tool is not able to trigger downloads on versions >= 0.0.19.\"\n\nWhile not directly applicable (the button wasn't triggering a download), this shows that `browser_click` has known issues with certain click actions.\n\n#### Variable Success Rate (Claude Code Issue #1383)\n\nFrom [anthropics/claude-code#1383](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/1383):\n\n> \"Playwright MCP success is... very variable. Sometimes it works great, it takes screenshots, it verifies its own work... then Claude is superbly useful. Other times it just can't seem to use playwright properly and so reverts to Curl.\"\n\nThis indicates that Playwright MCP reliability issues are a known problem.\n\n---\n\n### 5. AI Decision to Click Without Verification\n\n#### The Problem\n\nThe AI decided to click the \"Load data\" button without:\n1. Checking if the prerequisite data file existed\n2. Understanding the impact of loading 300K+ points\n3. Setting up any timeout or verification mechanism\n\n#### Evidence\n\nFrom the AI's message before the click:\n```\n\"Good! The page loaded. I can see there's a Cesium Ion 401 error (token issue)\nand a 404 for favicon. Let me click the 'Load Data' button to test data loading:\"\n```\n\nThe AI acknowledged errors but proceeded anyway.\n\n#### The Better Approach\n\nThe AI should have:\n1. Verified that `mrds.geojson` existed before clicking\n2. Noted that loading 304,613 points could be slow\n3. Used `browser_evaluate` to test data loading with a timeout\n4. Used `browser_wait_for` with a specific expected outcome\n\n#### The Fix\n\nAdd guidance to the AI system prompt:\n```markdown\nPlaywright MCP usage guidelines:\n- Before clicking buttons that load large datasets, verify the data source exists\n- For operations that may take long, use browser_evaluate with explicit timeouts\n- After clicks that trigger data loading, use browser_wait_for with expected text\n- Always have a fallback plan if browser operations don't complete in expected time\n```\n\n---\n\n## Root Cause Diagram\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                          SOLVE COMMAND STUCK                                 │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n                                    ▲\n                                    │\n              ┌─────────────────────┴─────────────────────┐\n              │                                           │\n              ▼                                           ▼\n┌─────────────────────────────┐           ┌─────────────────────────────┐\n│   Playwright MCP Config     │           │    solve.mjs Architecture   │\n│   (No timeout configured)   │           │    (No timeout detection)   │\n└─────────────────────────────┘           └─────────────────────────────┘\n              │                                           │\n              ▼                                           ▼\n┌─────────────────────────────┐           ┌─────────────────────────────┐\n│   browser_click hangs on    │           │   solve.mjs waits forever   │\n│   heavy JS operation        │           │   for tool response         │\n└─────────────────────────────┘           └─────────────────────────────┘\n              │                                           │\n              └───────────────────┬───────────────────────┘\n                                  │\n                                  ▼\n                    ┌─────────────────────────────┐\n                    │   No error, no timeout,     │\n                    │   manual Ctrl+C required    │\n                    └─────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThe root cause is a combination of factors:\n\n1. **Primary**: Missing timeout configuration in Playwright MCP\n2. **Secondary**: Heavy JavaScript operation blocking the browser\n3. **Tertiary**: No timeout detection in solve.mjs\n4. **Contributing**: AI clicking a button without proper verification\n\nAll of these issues contributed to the solve command becoming stuck for over 90 minutes with no error message or automatic recovery.\n",

      {

`https://github.com/${owner}/${repo}/issues/${issueNumber}` : `Issue linked to PR #${prNumber}`}`);\n  } else {\n    promptLines.push(`Issue to solve: ${issueUrl}`);\n  }\n\n  // Basic info\n  promptLines.push(`Your prepared branch: ${branchName}`);\n  promptLines.push(`Your prepared working directory: ${tempDir}`);\n\n  // PR info if available\n  if (prUrl) {\n    promptLines.push(`Your prepared Pull Request: ${prUrl}`);\n  }\n\n  // Fork info if applicable\n  if (argv && argv.fork && forkedRepo) {\n    promptLines.push(`Your forked repository: ${forkedRepo}`);\n    promptLines.push(`Original repository (upstream): ${owner}/${repo}`);\n\n    // Check for GitHub Actions on fork and add link if workflows exist\n    if (branchName && params.forkActionsUrl) {\n      promptLines.push(`GitHub Actions on your fork: ${params.forkActionsUrl}`);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Add contributing guidelines if available\n  if (contributingGuidelines) {\n    promptLines.push('');\n    promptLines.push(contributingGuidelines);\n  }\n\n  // Add blank line\n  promptLines.push('');\n\n  // Add feedback info if in continue mode and there are feedback items\n  if (isContinueMode && feedbackLines && feedbackLines.length > 0) {\n    // Add each feedback line directly\n    feedbackLines.forEach(line => promptLines.push(line));\n    promptLines.push('');\n  }\n\n  // Add thinking instruction based on --think level\n  if (argv && argv.think) {\n    const thinkMessages = {\n      low: 'Think.',\n      medium: 'Think hard.',\n      high: 'Think harder.',\n      max: 'Ultrathink.',\n    };\n    promptLines.push(thinkMessages[argv.think]);\n  }\n\n  // Final instruction\n  promptLines.push(isContinueMode ? You are polite and patient, and prefer to assume good intent, trying your best to be helpful. Based on research:\n\n### Primary Causes\n\n1. **Hardcoded 5-second MCP Ping Timeout** ([microsoft/playwright-mcp#982](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp/issues/982))\n   - When browser operations take longer than expected, the MCP server may terminate the session\n   - However, in this case, the session was NOT terminated - it simply hung\n\n2. **Click Action Triggering Long-Running Operation**\n   - The \"Load data\" button was loading 304,613 GeoJSON records\n   - This could trigger network requests, file loading, or JavaScript processing\n   - If the button triggered a file download or large data load, it may have caused Playwright to wait indefinitely\n\n3. **Known browser_click Issues** ([microsoft/playwright-mcp#355](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp/issues/355))\n   - Issues with `browser_click` triggering downloads on versions >= 0.0.19\n   - Click actions may not complete properly if they trigger asynchronous operations\n\n### Context from the Log\n\nThe page was a Cesium.js 3D globe application with:\n- Error: 401 on Cesium Ion API (token issue)\n- Error: 404 on mrds.geojson file\n- The \"Load data\" button was supposed to load mineral resource data points",
    "newString": "## Root Cause Analysis\n\nThe issue is related to the Playwright MCP tool not responding after a browser interaction. Based on research:\n\n### Primary Causes\n\n1. **Playwright MCP Timeout Not Being Respected**\n   - The Playwright MCP is configured with `--timeout-action=600000` (10 minutes)\n   - Despite this configuration, the action hung for 1 hour 34 minutes\n   - The timeout mechanism failed to trigger for unknown reasons\n\n2. **Click Action Triggering Long-Running Operation**\n   - The \"Load data\" button was loading 304,613 GeoJSON records\n   - This could trigger network requests, file loading, or JavaScript processing\n   - If the button triggered a file download or large data load, it may have caused Playwright to wait indefinitely\n\n3. **Known browser_click Issues** ([microsoft/playwright-mcp#355](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp/issues/355))\n   - Issues with `browser_click` triggering downloads on versions >= 0.0.19\n   - Click actions may not complete properly if they trigger asynchronous operations\n\n4. **MCP Protocol Layer Issue** ([microsoft/playwright-mcp#982](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp/issues/982))\n   - The timeout may apply to Playwright actions but not propagate through MCP protocol layer\n   - MCP connection remained active but no response was returned\n\n### Context from the Log\n\nThe page was a Cesium.js 3D globe application with:\n- Error: 401 on Cesium Ion API (token issue)\n- Error: 404 on mrds.geojson file\n- The \"Load data\" button was supposed to load mineral resource data points",
    "originalFile": "# Case Study: Solve Command Stuck on Playwright MCP Tool Call\n\n## Issue Summary\n\n**Issue**: [#1039](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1039)\n**Title**: Solve command stuck on Playwright MCP tool call\n**Date**: 2025-12-30\n**Status**: Analysis Complete\n\n## Problem Description\n\nThe `solve` command becomes stuck indefinitely when the AI assistant calls a Playwright MCP browser automation tool. In this specific case, the command was stuck for approximately **1 hour and 34 minutes** after calling `mcp__playwright__browser_click` to click a button on a web page.\n\n### Key Evidence\n\nFrom the log file `af1463d6-30a8-4119-92ff-d25a89bd948a.log`:\n\n- **11:22:53.672Z**: AI calls `mcp__playwright__browser_click` on \"Load data\" button (ref=e76)\n- **12:57:10.345Z**: Session interrupted by Ctrl+C (user intervention required)\n- **Gap**: ~1 hour 34 minutes with no response from Playwright MCP\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\n\nThe issue is related to the Playwright MCP tool not responding after a browser interaction. Based on research:\n\n### Primary Causes\n\n1. **Hardcoded 5-second MCP Ping Timeout** ([microsoft/playwright-mcp#982](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp/issues/982))\n   - When browser operations take longer than expected, the MCP server may terminate the session\n   - However, in this case, the session was NOT terminated - it simply hung\n\n2. **Click Action Triggering Long-Running Operation**\n   - The \"Load data\" button was loading 304,613 GeoJSON records\n   - This could trigger network requests, file loading, or JavaScript processing\n   - If the button triggered a file download or large data load, it may have caused Playwright to wait indefinitely\n\n3. **Known browser_click Issues** ([microsoft/playwright-mcp#355](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp/issues/355))\n   - Issues with `browser_click` triggering downloads on versions >= 0.0.19\n   - Click actions may not complete properly if they trigger asynchronous operations\n\n### Context from the Log\n\nThe page was a Cesium.js 3D globe application with:\n- Error: 401 on Cesium Ion API (token issue)\n- Error: 404 on mrds.geojson file\n- The \"Load data\" button was supposed to load mineral resource data points\n\n## Timeline of Events\n\n| Timestamp (UTC) | Event |\n|-----------------|-------|\n| 11:20:03.703 | Session starts, solve.mjs v0.51.18 |\n| 11:20:34.892 | Playwright MCP detected - browser hints enabled |\n| 11:20:37.889 | Claude session initialized, Playwright MCP connected |\n| 11:22:47.292 | AI navigates to http://localhost:8000 successfully |\n| 11:22:48.878 | Page snapshot shows UI with \"Load data\" button |\n| 11:22:53.672 | AI calls `browser_click` on \"Load data\" button (ref=e76) |\n| 12:57:10.345 | **Session interrupted (Ctrl+C)** - No response for 1h34m |\n\n## Files in This Case Study\n\n- `af1463d6-30a8-4119-92ff-d25a89bd948a.log` - Full log file from the stuck session\n- `README.md` - This overview document\n- `01-TIMELINE.md` - Detailed timeline reconstruction\n- `02-ROOT-CAUSES.md` - In-depth root cause analysis\n- `03-PROPOSED-SOLUTIONS.md` - Proposed solutions and mitigations\n\n## Proposed Solutions\n\n### 1. Add Action Timeout Configuration (Recommended)\nConfigure Playwright MCP with explicit timeout parameters:\n```bash\nclaude mcp add playwright -s user -- npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest \\\n  --isolated --headless --no-sandbox \\\n  --timeout-action=600000 \\\n  --viewport-size 1920x1080\n```\n\n### 2. Implement Click-with-Timeout Pattern\nInstead of using `browser_click` for buttons that trigger long operations:\n- Use `browser_snapshot` to verify the current state\n- Use `browser_evaluate` to execute JavaScript with explicit timeouts\n- Use `browser_wait_for` with specific text expectations\n\n### 3. Add Monitoring and Recovery\n- Implement timeout detection in solve.mjs for MCP tool calls\n- Add automatic recovery mechanism when tools don't respond\n- Log warning when MCP tools exceed expected duration\n\n### 4. AI Prompt Enhancement\nAdd guidance to the system prompt:\n- Avoid clicking buttons that trigger large data downloads without first verifying the download target\n- Use `browser_wait_for` after clicks that trigger data loading\n- Always close the browser when done to free resources\n\n## Related Issues and Resources\n\n### Playwright MCP Issues\n- [microsoft/playwright-mcp#982](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp/issues/982) - MCP ping timeout configuration\n- [microsoft/playwright-mcp#355](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp/issues/355) - browser_click download issues\n- [microsoft/playwright-mcp#1082](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp/issues/1082) - MCP client timeout issues\n\n### Claude Code Issues\n- [anthropics/claude-code#1383](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/1383) - Playwright MCP frequently fails\n\n### Previous Case Studies\n- [issue-837-playwright-mcp-chrome-leak](../issue-837-playwright-mcp-chrome-leak/) - Playwright MCP memory leak and configuration\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThe issue appears to be caused by the Playwright MCP tool hanging after a `browser_click` action that triggered a long-running operation (loading 300K+ data points). The click action never returned a response, leaving the solve command stuck until manual intervention.\n\nThe recommended fix is to:\n1. Configure Playwright MCP with proper timeout settings\n2. Enhance the AI system prompt with guidance on handling potentially long-running browser operations\n3. Consider implementing timeout detection at the solve.mjs level for MCP tool calls\n",

In this specific case, the command was stuck for approximately **1 hour and 34 minutes** after calling `mcp__playwright__browser_click` to click a button on a web page.\n\n### Key Evidence\n\nFrom the log file `af1463d6-30a8-4119-92ff-d25a89bd948a.log`:\n\n- **11:22:53.672Z**: AI calls `mcp__playwright__browser_click` on \"Load data\" button (ref=e76)\n- **12:57:10.345Z**: Session interrupted by Ctrl+C (user intervention required)\n- **Gap**: ~1 hour 34 minutes with no response from Playwright MCP\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\n\nThe issue is related to the Playwright MCP tool not responding after a browser interaction. Based on research:\n\n### Primary Causes\n\n1. **Playwright MCP Timeout Not Being Respected**\n   - The Playwright MCP is configured with `--timeout-action=600000` (10 minutes)\n   - Despite this configuration, the action hung for 1 hour 34 minutes\n   - The timeout mechanism failed to trigger for unknown reasons\n\n2. **Click Action Triggering Long-Running Operation**\n   - The \"Load data\" button was loading 304,613 GeoJSON records\n   - This could trigger network requests, file loading, or JavaScript processing\n   - If the button triggered a file download or large data load, it may have caused Playwright to wait indefinitely\n\n3. **Known browser_click Issues** ([microsoft/playwright-mcp#355](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp/issues/355))\n   - Issues with `browser_click` triggering downloads on versions >= 0.0.19\n   - Click actions may not complete properly if they trigger asynchronous operations\n\n4. **MCP Protocol Layer Issue** ([microsoft/playwright-mcp#982](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp/issues/982))\n   - The timeout may apply to Playwright actions but not propagate through MCP protocol layer\n   - MCP connection remained active but no response was returned\n\n### Context from the Log\n\nThe page was a Cesium.js 3D globe application with:\n- Error: 401 on Cesium Ion API (token issue)\n- Error: 404 on mrds.geojson file\n- The \"Load data\" button was supposed to load mineral resource data points\n\n## Timeline of Events\n\n| Timestamp (UTC) | Event |\n|-----------------|-------|\n| 11:20:03.703 | Session starts, solve.mjs v0.51.18 |\n| 11:20:34.892 | Playwright MCP detected - browser hints enabled |\n| 11:20:37.889 | Claude session initialized, Playwright MCP connected |\n| 11:22:47.292 | AI navigates to http://localhost:8000 successfully |\n| 11:22:48.878 | Page snapshot shows UI with \"Load data\" button |\n| 11:22:53.672 | AI calls `browser_click` on \"Load data\" button (ref=e76) |\n| 12:57:10.345 | **Session interrupted (Ctrl+C)** - No response for 1h34m |\n\n## Files in This Case Study\n\n- `af1463d6-30a8-4119-92ff-d25a89bd948a.log` - Full log file from the stuck session\n- `README.md` - This overview document\n- `01-TIMELINE.md` - Detailed timeline reconstruction\n- `02-ROOT-CAUSES.md` - In-depth root cause analysis\n- `03-PROPOSED-SOLUTIONS.md` - Proposed solutions and mitigations\n\n## Proposed Solutions\n\n### 1. Add Action Timeout Configuration (Recommended)\nConfigure Playwright MCP with explicit timeout parameters:\n```bash\nclaude mcp add playwright -s user -- npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest \\\n  --isolated --headless --no-sandbox \\\n  --timeout-action=600000 \\\n  --viewport-size 1920x1080\n```\n\n### 2. Implement Click-with-Timeout Pattern\nInstead of using `browser_click` for buttons that trigger long operations:\n- Use `browser_snapshot` to verify the current state\n- Use `browser_evaluate` to execute JavaScript with explicit timeouts\n- Use `browser_wait_for` with specific text expectations\n\n### 3. Add Monitoring and Recovery\n- Implement timeout detection in solve.mjs for MCP tool calls\n- Add automatic recovery mechanism when tools don't respond\n- Log warning when MCP tools exceed expected duration\n\n### 4. AI Prompt Enhancement\nAdd guidance to the system prompt:\n- Avoid clicking buttons that trigger large data downloads without first verifying the download target\n- Use `browser_wait_for` after clicks that trigger data loading\n- Always close the browser when done to free resources\n\n## Related Issues and Resources\n\n### Playwright MCP Issues\n- [microsoft/playwright-mcp#982](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp/issues/982) - MCP ping timeout configuration\n- [microsoft/playwright-mcp#355](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp/issues/355) - browser_click download issues\n- [microsoft/playwright-mcp#1082](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp/issues/1082) - MCP client timeout issues\n\n### Claude Code Issues\n- [anthropics/claude-code#1383](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/1383) - Playwright MCP frequently fails\n\n### Previous Case Studies\n- [issue-837-playwright-mcp-chrome-leak](../issue-837-playwright-mcp-chrome-leak/) - Playwright MCP memory leak and configuration\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThe issue appears to be caused by the Playwright MCP tool hanging after a `browser_click` action that triggered a long-running operation (loading 300K+ data points). The click action never returned a response, leaving the solve command stuck until manual intervention.\n\nThe recommended fix is to:\n1. Configure Playwright MCP with proper timeout settings\n2. Enhance the AI system prompt with guidance on handling potentially long-running browser operations\n3. Consider implementing timeout detection at the solve.mjs level for MCP tool calls\n",

          "new_string": "## Solution Overview\n\n| Solution | Complexity | Impact | Priority | Status |\n|----------|------------|--------|----------|--------|\n| 1. Enhance AI system prompt | Low | Medium | P0 - Critical | **IMPLEMENTED** |\n| 2. Add timeout detection in solve.mjs | Medium | High | P1 - High | Pending |\n| 3. Implement auto-recovery mechanism | High | Medium | P3 - Low | Pending |\n\n## Solution 1: Enhance AI System Prompt (P0) - IMPLEMENTED\n\n### The Change\n\nAdded safety guidelines to the Playwright MCP section of the AI system prompt to help prevent clicking buttons that trigger long operations.\n\n### Implementation Details\n\nUpdated `src/claude.prompts.lib.mjs` to add the following guidelines:\n\n```markdown\n- IMPORTANT: Before clicking buttons that may trigger large data operations (loading thousands of records, heavy computations), verify the operation is safe by checking if data sources exist and considering the operation time.\n- IMPORTANT: If console errors show 401/403/404 errors, address authentication or missing resource issues before testing UI functionality.\n- IMPORTANT: After clicking buttons that trigger data loading, use browser_wait_for with specific expected text to verify completion, or use browser_snapshot periodically to check progress.\n- IMPORTANT: For potentially long-running operations, prefer browser_evaluate with explicit JavaScript timeouts over browser_click to maintain control.\n```\n\n### Why This Is P0\n\nThe timeout configuration already exists (`--timeout-action=600000`) but is not being respected. The most immediate and practical fix is to give the AI better guidance to avoid triggering operations that may cause hangs.\n\n---\n\n## Note on Playwright MCP Timeout Configuration\n\n### Current Configuration (ubuntu-24-server-install.sh)\n\n```bash\nclaude mcp add playwright -s user -- npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest \\\n  --isolated --headless --no-sandbox \\\n  --timeout-action=600000 \\\n  --viewport-size 1920x1080\n```\n\n### The Problem\n\nThe current configuration already includes `--timeout-action=600000` (10 minutes). However, in the issue case, the operation hung for **1 hour 34 minutes** without triggering the timeout. This suggests:\n\n1. The timeout may not apply to all browser actions\n2. The MCP protocol layer may not properly propagate timeout errors\n3. When browser JavaScript blocks the main thread, the timeout detection may fail\n\n### Recommendation\n\nConsider opening an issue on [microsoft/playwright-mcp](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp) to investigate why the timeout is not being respected.\n\n---"

Configure Playwright MCP with timeouts | Low | High | P0 - Critical |\n| 2. Add timeout detection in solve.mjs | Medium | High | P1 - High |\n| 3. Enhance AI system prompt | Low | Medium | P2 - Medium |\n| 4. Implement auto-recovery mechanism | High | Medium | P3 - Low |\n\n## Solution 1: Configure Playwright MCP with Timeouts (P0)\n\n### The Change\n\nUpdate the Playwright MCP configuration to include explicit timeout settings.\n\n### Current Configuration (ubuntu-24-server-install.sh)\n\n```bash\nclaude mcp add playwright -s user -- npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest \\\n  --isolated --headless --no-sandbox \\\n  --timeout-action=600000 \\\n  --viewport-size 1920x1080\n```\n\n### Verification\n\nThe current configuration already includes `--timeout-action=600000` (10 minutes). However, we should verify:\n\n1. This timeout is being respected by the MCP server\n2. The timeout applies to all browser actions including `click()`\n3. The timeout generates an appropriate error message when triggered\n\n### Additional Recommendations\n\nConsider adding these parameters:\n\n```bash\nclaude mcp add playwright -s user -- npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest \\\n  --isolated \\\n  --headless \\\n  --no-sandbox \\\n  --timeout-action=600000 \\\n  --timeout-navigation=120000 \\\n  --viewport-size 1920x1080\n```\n\nParameters explained:\n- `--timeout-action=600000`: 10 minute timeout for actions (click, type, etc.)\n- `--timeout-navigation=120000`: 2 minute timeout for page navigation\n- `--isolated`: Each session gets a fresh browser context\n- `--headless`: Run without visible browser window\n- `--no-sandbox`: Required for running as root in Docker/CI\n\n---\n\n## Solution 2: Add Timeout Detection in solve.mjs (P1)\n\n### The Problem\n\nsolve.mjs currently has no mechanism to detect or handle hung MCP tool calls.\n\n### Proposed Implementation\n\nAdd a wrapper function that tracks tool execution time and implements timeout logic:\n\n```javascript\n// In solve.mjs or a new module\n\nconst TOOL_TIMEOUTS = {\n  'mcp__playwright__browser_navigate': 120000,    // 2 minutes for navigation\n  'mcp__playwright__browser_click': 60000,        // 1 minute for clicks\n  'mcp__playwright__browser_type': 60000,         // 1 minute for typing\n  'mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot': 30000,     // 30 seconds for snapshots\n  'mcp__playwright__browser_evaluate': 300000,    // 5 minutes for JS execution\n  'default': 120000                               // 2 minutes default\n};\n\nconst WARNING_THRESHOLD = 30000; // Warn after 30 seconds\n\nasync function trackToolExecution(toolName, toolPromise) {\n  const startTime = Date.now();\n  const timeout = TOOL_TIMEOUTS[toolName] || TOOL_TIMEOUTS.default;\n\n  const warningInterval = setInterval(() => {\n    const elapsed = Date.now() - startTime;\n    if (elapsed > WARNING_THRESHOLD) {\n      log(`⚠️ Tool ${toolName} running for ${Math.round(elapsed / 1000)}s...`);\n    }\n  }, 10000); // Log every 10 seconds\n\n  try {\n    const timeoutPromise = new Promise((_, reject) => {\n      setTimeout(() => {\n        reject(new Error(`Tool ${toolName} timed out after ${timeout}ms`));\n      }, timeout);\n    });\n\n    return await Promise.race([toolPromise, timeoutPromise]);\n  } finally {\n    clearInterval(warningInterval);\n    const duration = Date.now() - startTime;\n    log(`📊 Tool ${toolName} completed in ${Math.round(duration / 1000)}s`);\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Integration Points\n\n1. Wrap Claude API tool call handling with timeout tracking\n2. Log warnings for slow-running tools\n3. Implement graceful error handling for timeouts\n\n---\n\n## Solution 3: Enhance AI System Prompt (P2)\n\n### Current Prompt Section\n\nFrom `src/claude.prompts.lib.mjs`:\n```markdown\nPlaywright MCP usage (browser automation via mcp__playwright__* tools).\n   - When you develop frontend web applications (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, Angular, etc.), use Playwright MCP tools to test the UI in a real browser.\n   - When WebFetch tool fails to retrieve expected content (e.g., returns empty content, JavaScript-rendered pages, or login-protected pages), use Playwright MCP tools (browser_navigate, browser_snapshot) as a fallback for web browsing.\n   - When you need to interact with dynamic web pages that require JavaScript execution, use Playwright MCP tools.\n   - When you need to visually verify how a web page looks or take screenshots, use brows*************nshot from Playwright MCP.\n   - When you need to fill forms, click buttons, or perform user interactions on web pages, use Playwright MCP tools (browser_click, browser_type, browser_fill_form).\n   - When you need to test responsive design or different viewport sizes, use browser_resize from Playwright MCP.\n   - When you finish using the browser, always close it with browser_close to free resources.\n```\n\n### Proposed Additions\n\nAdd these guidelines to prevent stuck operations:\n\n```markdown\nPlaywright MCP safety guidelines:\n   - Before clicking buttons that may trigger large data operations, check if the data source exists and estimate the operation time.\n   - For pages that load large datasets (e.g., GeoJSON with thousands of features), consider using browser_snapshot to verify state before and after interactions.\n   - After clicking buttons that trigger data loading, use browser_wait_for with a specific expected text or state change, or take periodic snapshots to verify progress.\n   - If a browser operation may take longer than expected, prefer browser_evaluate with explicit JavaScript timeouts over browser_click.\n   - When errors occur (like 401 or 404), consider investigating the cause before proceeding with other interactions.\n   - If console errors indicate missing resources (like API tokens or data files), address those issues before testing UI functionality.\n```\n\n### Example Good Pattern\n\n```markdown\nExample: Testing a data loading button\n1. First, verify the data file exists: `browser_network_requests` or check file system\n2. Take a snapshot to verify current state: `browser_snapshot`\n3. Use browser_evaluate to click with timeout:\n   `browser_evaluate({ function: \"() => { document.querySelector('#loadBtn').click(); return 'clicked'; }\" })`\n4. Use browser_wait_for to verify data loaded:\n   `browser_wait_for({ text: \"Loaded 1000 items\" })`\n5. Take final snapshot to verify: `browser_snapshot`\n```\n\n---\n\n## Solution 4: Implement Auto-Recovery Mechanism (P3)\n\n### Overview\n\nImplement an auto-recovery mechanism that can detect and recover from hung browser operations.\n\n### Architecture\n\n```\n┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐\n│   solve.mjs      │────▶│  Tool Executor   │────▶│  Playwright MCP  │\n│                  │     │  (with timeout)  │     │                  │\n└──────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘\n                               │    ▲\n                               ▼    │\n                         ┌──────────────────┐\n                         │  Recovery Agent  │\n                         │  - Kill browser  │\n                         │  - Restart MCP   │\n                         │  - Retry action  │\n                         └──────────────────┘\n```\n\n### Implementation Sketch\n\n```javascript\nclass Brows**********Agent {\n  constructor(options = {}) {\n    this.maxRetries = options.maxRetries || 3;\n    this.retryDelay = options.retryDelay || 5000;\n  }\n\n  async executeWithRecovery(toolCall) {\n    for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= this.maxRetries; attempt++) {\n      try {\n        return await this.executeWithTimeout(toolCall);\n      } catch (error) {\n        if (error.message.includes('timed out') && attempt < this.maxRetries) {\n          log(`⚠️ Attempt ${attempt} failed, recovering...`);\n          await this.recover();\n          await this.delay(this.retryDelay);\n        } else {\n          throw error;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  async recover() {\n    // 1. Try to close the browser gracefully\n    try {\n      await this.callTool('mcp__playwright__browser_close');\n    } catch (e) {\n      log('Browser close failed, forcing...');\n    }\n\n    // 2. Kill any orphaned browser processes\n    await this.killOrphanedBrowsers();\n\n    // 3. Restart MCP connection if needed\n    await this.restartMcpConnection();\n  }\n\n  async killOrphanedBrowsers() {\n    // Find and kill orphaned Chromium processes\n    const { exec } = require('child_process');\n    exec('pkill -f chromium --signal KILL', (err) => {\n      if (err) log('No orphaned browsers to kill');\n    });\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Considerations\n\n- This is a more complex solution that requires careful testing\n- May not be needed if Solutions 1-3 are implemented properly\n- Could be useful for long-running automated sessions\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Priority\n\n### Phase 1: Immediate (P0)\n- [x] Verify Playwright MCP configuration has proper timeouts\n- [ ] Test that timeouts are being respected\n- [ ] Document any configuration changes needed\n\n### Phase 2: Short-term (P1)\n- [ ] Add timeout tracking to solve.mjs tool execution\n- [ ] Implement warning logs for slow operations\n- [ ] Add graceful error handling for timeout errors\n\n### Phase 3: Medium-term (P2)\n- [ ] Update AI system prompt with safety guidelines\n- [ ] Add examples of safe browser interaction patterns\n- [ ] Create documentation for Playwright MCP best practices\n\n### Phase 4: Long-term (P3)\n- [ ] Design auto-recovery mechanism\n- [ ] Implement browser process management\n- [ ] Add comprehensive logging and monitoring\n\n## Testing Plan\n\n### Test Case 1: Timeout Verification\n1. Configure Playwright MCP with `--timeout-action=10000` (10 seconds)\n2. Navigate to a page with a button that triggers a 30-second operation\n3. Click the button\n4. **Expected**: Error after 10 seconds, not a hang\n\n### Test Case 2: solve.mjs Timeout Detection\n1. Enable timeout tracking in solve.mjs\n2. Create a mock MCP server that delays responses\n3. **Expected**: Warning logs after 30 seconds, error after timeout\n\n### Test Case 3: AI Prompt Effectiveness\n1. Present AI with a scenario involving large data load\n2. **Expected**: AI should take precautions before clicking\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThe recommended approach is to implement Solutions 1-3 immediately:\n\n1. **Verify and update Playwright MCP configuration** to ensure timeouts are properly set and respected\n2. **Add timeout detection to solve.mjs** to catch and handle hung operations\n3. **Enhance the AI system prompt** to prevent problematic browser interactions\n\nThese three solutions together should prevent future occurrences of the solve command becoming stuck on Playwright MCP tool calls.\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":4,"oldLines":57,"newStart":4,"newLines":59,"lines":[" "," ## Solution Overview"," ","-| Solution | Complexity | Impact | Priority |","-|----------|------------|--------|----------|","-| 1.     "oldString": "## Solution 3: Enhance AI System Prompt (P2)",
    "newString": "## Solution 3: Report Issue to Playwright MCP (P2)",
    "originalFile": "# Proposed Solutions: Solve Command Stuck on Playwright MCP Tool Call\n\nThis document outlines proposed solutions to prevent the solve command from becoming stuck on Playwright MCP tool calls.\n\n## Solution Overview\n\n| Solution | Complexity | Impact | Priority | Status |\n|----------|------------|--------|----------|--------|\n| 1. Enhance AI system prompt | Low | Medium | P0 - Critical | **IMPLEMENTED** |\n| 2. Add timeout detection in solve.mjs | Medium | High | P1 - High | Pending |\n| 3. Report issue to Playwright MCP | Low | High | P2 - Medium | Pending |\n| 4. Implement auto-recovery mechanism | High | Medium | P3 - Low | Pending |\n\n## Solution 1: Enhance AI System Prompt (P0) - IMPLEMENTED\n\n### The Change\n\nAdded safety guidelines to the Playwright MCP section of the AI system prompt to help prevent clicking buttons that trigger long operations.\n\n### Implementation Details\n\nUpdated `src/claude.prompts.lib.mjs` to add the following guidelines:\n\n```markdown\n- IMPORTANT: Before clicking buttons that may trigger large data operations (loading thousands of records, heavy computations), verify the operation is safe by checking if data sources exist and considering the operation time.\n- IMPORTANT: If console errors show 401/403/404 errors, address authentication or missing resource issues before testing UI functionality.\n- IMPORTANT: After clicking buttons that trigger data loading, use browser_wait_for with specific expected text to verify completion, or use browser_snapshot periodically to check progress.\n- IMPORTANT: For potentially long-running operations, prefer browser_evaluate with explicit JavaScript timeouts over browser_click to maintain control.\n```\n\n### Why This Is P0\n\nThe timeout configuration already exists (`--timeout-action=600000`) but is not being respected. The most immediate and practical fix is to give the AI better guidance to avoid triggering operations that may cause hangs.\n\n---\n\n## Note on Playwright MCP Timeout Configuration\n\n### Current Configuration (ubuntu-24-server-install.sh)\n\n```bash\nclaude mcp add playwright -s user -- npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest \\\n  --isolated --headless --no-sandbox \\\n  --timeout-action=600000 \\\n  --viewport-size 1920x1080\n```\n\n### The Problem\n\nThe current configuration already includes `--timeout-action=600000` (10 minutes). However, in the issue case, the operation hung for **1 hour 34 minutes** without triggering the timeout. This suggests:\n\n1. The timeout may not apply to all browser actions\n2. The MCP protocol layer may not properly propagate timeout errors\n3. When browser JavaScript blocks the main thread, the timeout detection may fail\n\n### Recommendation\n\nConsider opening an issue on [microsoft/playwright-mcp](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp) to investigate why the timeout is not being respected.\n\n---\n\n## Solution 2: Add Timeout Detection in solve.mjs (P1)\n\n### The Problem\n\nsolve.mjs currently has no mechanism to detect or handle hung MCP tool calls.\n\n### Proposed Implementation\n\nAdd a wrapper function that tracks tool execution time and implements timeout logic:\n\n```javascript\n// In solve.mjs or a new module\n\nconst TOOL_TIMEOUTS = {\n  'mcp__playwright__browser_navigate': 120000,    // 2 minutes for navigation\n  'mcp__playwright__browser_click': 60000,        // 1 minute for clicks\n  'mcp__playwright__browser_type': 60000,         // 1 minute for typing\n  'mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot': 30000,     // 30 seconds for snapshots\n  'mcp__playwright__browser_evaluate': 300000,    // 5 minutes for JS execution\n  'default': 120000                               // 2 minutes default\n};\n\nconst WARNING_THRESHOLD = 30000; // Warn after 30 seconds\n\nasync function trackToolExecution(toolName, toolPromise) {\n  const startTime = Date.now();\n  const timeout = TOOL_TIMEOUTS[toolName] || TOOL_TIMEOUTS.default;\n\n  const warningInterval = setInterval(() => {\n    const elapsed = Date.now() - startTime;\n    if (elapsed > WARNING_THRESHOLD) {\n      log(`⚠️ Tool ${toolName} running for ${Math.round(elapsed / 1000)}s...`);\n    }\n  }, 10000); // Log every 10 seconds\n\n  try {\n    const timeoutPromise = new Promise((_, reject) => {\n      setTimeout(() => {\n        reject(new Error(`Tool ${toolName} timed out after ${timeout}ms`));\n      }, timeout);\n    });\n\n    return await Promise.race([toolPromise, timeoutPromise]);\n  } finally {\n    clearInterval(warningInterval);\n    const duration = Date.now() - startTime;\n    log(`📊 Tool ${toolName} completed in ${Math.round(duration / 1000)}s`);\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Integration Points\n\n1. Wrap Claude API tool call handling with timeout tracking\n2. Log warnings for slow-running tools\n3. Implement graceful error handling for timeouts\n\n---\n\n## Solution 3: Enhance AI System Prompt (P2)\n\n### Current Prompt Section\n\nFrom `src/claude.prompts.lib.mjs`:\n```markdown\nPlaywright MCP usage (browser automation via mcp__playwright__* tools).\n   - When you develop frontend web applications (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, Angular, etc.), use Playwright MCP tools to test the UI in a real browser.\n   - When WebFetch tool fails to retrieve expected content (e.g., returns empty content, JavaScript-rendered pages, or login-protected pages), use Playwright MCP tools (browser_navigate, browser_snapshot) as a fallback for web browsing.\n   - When you need to interact with dynamic web pages that require JavaScript execution, use Playwright MCP tools.\n   - When you need to visually verify how a web page looks or take screenshots, use brows*************nshot from Playwright MCP.\n   - When you need to fill forms, click buttons, or perform user interactions on web pages, use Playwright MCP tools (browser_click, browser_type, browser_fill_form).\n   - When you need to test responsive design or different viewport sizes, use browser_resize from Playwright MCP.\n   - When you finish using the browser, always close it with browser_close to free resources.\n```\n\n### Proposed Additions\n\nAdd these guidelines to prevent stuck operations:\n\n```markdown\nPlaywright MCP safety guidelines:\n   - Before clicking buttons that may trigger large data operations, check if the data source exists and estimate the operation time.\n   - For pages that load large datasets (e.g., GeoJSON with thousands of features), consider using browser_snapshot to verify state before and after interactions.\n   - After clicking buttons that trigger data loading, use browser_wait_for with a specific expected text or state change, or take periodic snapshots to verify progress.\n   - If a browser operation may take longer than expected, prefer browser_evaluate with explicit JavaScript timeouts over browser_click.\n   - When errors occur (like 401 or 404), consider investigating the cause before proceeding with other interactions.\n   - If console errors indicate missing resources (like API tokens or data files), address those issues before testing UI functionality.\n```\n\n### Example Good Pattern\n\n```markdown\nExample: Testing a data loading button\n1. First, verify the data file exists: `browser_network_requests` or check file system\n2. Take a snapshot to verify current state: `browser_snapshot`\n3. Use browser_evaluate to click with timeout:\n   `browser_evaluate({ function: \"() => { document.querySelector('#loadBtn').click(); return 'clicked'; }\" })`\n4. Use browser_wait_for to verify data loaded:\n   `browser_wait_for({ text: \"Loaded 1000 items\" })`\n5. Take final snapshot to verify: `browser_snapshot`\n```\n\n---\n\n## Solution 4: Implement Auto-Recovery Mechanism (P3)\n\n### Overview\n\nImplement an auto-recovery mechanism that can detect and recover from hung browser operations.\n\n### Architecture\n\n```\n┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐\n│   solve.mjs      │────▶│  Tool Executor   │────▶│  Playwright MCP  │\n│                  │     │  (with timeout)  │     │                  │\n└──────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘\n                               │    ▲\n                               ▼    │\n                         ┌──────────────────┐\n                         │  Recovery Agent  │\n                         │  - Kill browser  │\n                         │  - Restart MCP   │\n                         │  - Retry action  │\n                         └──────────────────┘\n```\n\n### Implementation Sketch\n\n```javascript\nclass Brows**********Agent {\n  constructor(options = {}) {\n    this.maxRetries = options.maxRetries || 3;\n    this.retryDelay = options.retryDelay || 5000;\n  }\n\n  async executeWithRecovery(toolCall) {\n    for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= this.maxRetries; attempt++) {\n      try {\n        return await this.executeWithTimeout(toolCall);\n      } catch (error) {\n        if (error.message.includes('timed out') && attempt < this.maxRetries) {\n          log(`⚠️ Attempt ${attempt} failed, recovering...`);\n          await this.recover();\n          await this.delay(this.retryDelay);\n        } else {\n          throw error;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  async recover() {\n    // 1. Try to close the browser gracefully\n    try {\n      await this.callTool('mcp__playwright__browser_close');\n    } catch (e) {\n      log('Browser close failed, forcing...');\n    }\n\n    // 2. Kill any orphaned browser processes\n    await this.killOrphanedBrowsers();\n\n    // 3. Restart MCP connection if needed\n    await this.restartMcpConnection();\n  }\n\n  async killOrphanedBrowsers() {\n    // Find and kill orphaned Chromium processes\n    const { exec } = require('child_process');\n    exec('pkill -f chromium --signal KILL', (err) => {\n      if (err) log('No orphaned browsers to kill');\n    });\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Considerations\n\n- This is a more complex solution that requires careful testing\n- May not be needed if Solutions 1-3 are implemented properly\n- Could be useful for long-running automated sessions\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Priority\n\n### Phase 1: Immediate (P0)\n- [x] Verify Playwright MCP configuration has proper timeouts\n- [ ] Test that timeouts are being respected\n- [ ] Document any configuration changes needed\n\n### Phase 2: Short-term (P1)\n- [ ] Add timeout tracking to solve.mjs tool execution\n- [ ] Implement warning logs for slow operations\n- [ ] Add graceful error handling for timeout errors\n\n### Phase 3: Medium-term (P2)\n- [ ] Update AI system prompt with safety guidelines\n- [ ] Add examples of safe browser interaction patterns\n- [ ] Create documentation for Playwright MCP best practices\n\n### Phase 4: Long-term (P3)\n- [ ] Design auto-recovery mechanism\n- [ ] Implement browser process management\n- [ ] Add comprehensive logging and monitoring\n\n## Testing Plan\n\n### Test Case 1: Timeout Verification\n1. Configure Playwright MCP with `--timeout-action=10000` (10 seconds)\n2. Navigate to a page with a button that triggers a 30-second operation\n3. Click the button\n4. **Expected**: Error after 10 seconds, not a hang\n\n### Test Case 2: solve.mjs Timeout Detection\n1. Enable timeout tracking in solve.mjs\n2. Create a mock MCP server that delays responses\n3. **Expected**: Warning logs after 30 seconds, error after timeout\n\n### Test Case 3: AI Prompt Effectiveness\n1. Present AI with a scenario involving large data load\n2. **Expected**: AI should take precautions before clicking\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThe recommended approach is to implement Solutions 1-3 immediately:\n\n1. **Verify and update Playwright MCP configuration** to ensure timeouts are properly set and respected\n2. **Add timeout detection to solve.mjs** to catch and handle hung operations\n3. **Enhance the AI system prompt** to prevent problematic browser interactions\n\nThese three solutions together should prevent future occurrences of the solve command becoming stuck on Playwright MCP tool calls.\n",

Before clicking buttons that may trigger large data operations, check if the data source exists and estimate the operation time.\n   - For pages that load large datasets (e.g., GeoJSON with thousands of features), consider using browser_snapshot to verify state before and after interactions.\n   - After clicking buttons that trigger data loading, use browser_wait_for with a specific expected text or state change, or take periodic snapshots to verify progress.\n   - If a browser operation may take longer than expected, prefer browser_evaluate with explicit JavaScript timeouts over browser_click.\n   - When errors occur (like 401 or 404), consider investigating the cause before proceeding with other interactions.\n   - If console errors indicate missing resources (like API tokens or data files), address those issues before testing UI functionality.\n```\n\n### Example Good Pattern\n\n```markdown\nExample: Testing a data loading button\n1. First, verify the data file exists: `browser_network_requests` or check file system\n2. Take a snapshot to verify current state: `browser_snapshot`\n3. Use browser_evaluate to click with timeout:\n   `browser_evaluate({ function: \"() => { document.querySelector('#loadBtn').click(); return 'clicked'; }\" })`\n4. Use browser_wait_for to verify data loaded:\n   `browser_wait_for({ text: \"Loaded 1000 items\" })`\n5. Take final snapshot to verify: `browser_snapshot`\n```\n\n---\n\n## Solution 4: Implement Auto-Recovery Mechanism (P3)\n\n### Overview\n\nImplement an auto-recovery mechanism that can detect and recover from hung browser operations.\n\n### Architecture\n\n```\n┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐\n│   solve.mjs      │────▶│  Tool Executor   │────▶│  Playwright MCP  │\n│                  │     │  (with timeout)  │     │                  │\n└──────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘\n                               │    ▲\n                               ▼    │\n                         ┌──────────────────┐\n                         │  Recovery Agent  │\n                         │  - Kill browser  │\n                         │  - Restart MCP   │\n                         │  - Retry action  │\n                         └──────────────────┘\n```\n\n### Implementation Sketch\n\n```javascript\nclass Brows**********Agent {\n  constructor(options = {}) {\n    this.maxRetries = options.maxRetries || 3;\n    this.retryDelay = options.retryDelay || 5000;\n  }\n\n  async executeWithRecovery(toolCall) {\n    for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= this.maxRetries; attempt++) {\n      try {\n        return await this.executeWithTimeout(toolCall);\n      } catch (error) {\n        if (error.message.includes('timed out') && attempt < this.maxRetries) {\n          log(`⚠️ Attempt ${attempt} failed, recovering...`);\n          await this.recover();\n          await this.delay(this.retryDelay);\n        } else {\n          throw error;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  async recover() {",

    }
